message:
perf(query): streaming COUNT+SUM aggregate — eliminate intermediate allocation (bd-wwqen.4)

Replace the two-phase COUNT+SUM fast path (collect all values into Vec, then
compute aggregate) with a single-pass streaming approach that calls
sum_func.step() directly per-row. This avoids allocating a Vec<SqliteValue>
and a Vec<&SqliteValue> for the intermediate collection, matching SQLite's
native streaming aggregate semantics where SUM accumulates incrementally.

The sum aggregate function is now resolved once from the function registry
at the start of the scan, and its state is stepped inline during the row
iteration loop. This is both more memory-efficient (O(1) vs O(N)) and
slightly faster due to eliminated allocation overhead.

Also updates beads issue tracker with status changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
